page: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes a 40 MB L3 cache and a massive memory allocation of 448.0 GB, yielding 28.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 918 GB drive, while it contains no dedicated graphics processing units.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core capabilities of the Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 are limited, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the middle 50%, supporting the massive RAM capacity. Large-model text generation is a bottleneck, with LLM text generation for both 135M and 7B models falling into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS5-16 offers a high density of memory relative to compute resources, making it a specialized option where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw CPU speed. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory footprints but do not demand high single-threaded CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 448.0 GB of RAM, providing 28.0 GB per core, alongside 918 GB of local storage and no GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, scoring in the bottom 10% to 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth consistently reach average levels. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute power. It is suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 448 GB of RAM, and 918 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core and memory performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon cores for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

448.0 GB of system memory providing 28.0 GB of RAM per core
918 GB of bundled local storage with no GPU accelerators
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
